name: Pocket Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 2 cups of whole-wheat flour and the active dry yeast.
2. In a separate container, dissolve 1 tablespoon of honey in 2 cups of very warm water.
3. Add the honey-water mixture to the flour-yeast mixture. Mix for 2 minutes at medium speed with an electric mixer, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally. Alternatively, mix by hand for about 150 strokes.
4. Gradually add enough whole-wheat flour to form a soft dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
5. Knead until smooth and elastic, approximately 8-10 minutes, adding more flour if needed.
6. Place the dough in a greased bowl. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
7. Gently punch down the dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
8. Cover again and let rest for 30 minutes.
9. Divide the dough into 10 equal pieces. Shape each piece into a ball.
10. On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball into a 6-inch circle.
11. Place the circles on a lightly floured baking sheet.
12. Bake on the bottom rack of a preheated oven set to 450°F for about 5 minutes. The tops will not be browned.
13. To achieve brown tops, place under a broiler about 3 inches from the heat for 1 minute.
